front: Define an Euler path in a graph
back: An Euler path is a trail in a graph that visits every edge exactly once. It starts at one vertex and ends at a different vertex.
An Euler circuit (or Euler cycle) is a closed trail that visits every edge of a graph exactly once and returns to the starting vertex.
A graph containing an Euler circuit (i.e., a closed Eulerian path) is called an Eulerian graph. Every vertex must have even degree.
A graph that has an Euler path but not an Euler circuit is called Semi-Eulerian. It has exactly two vertices of odd degree.

front: State the necessary and sufficient condition for a connected graph to be Eulerian
back: A connected graph is Eulerian if and only if every vertex has even degree.

front: State the condition for a connected graph to have an Euler path but not circuit
back: A connected graph has an Euler path (but not circuit) if and only if it has exactly two vertices of odd degree.

front: What is Fleury's algorithm used for?
back: Fleury's algorithm is used to find an Euler path or circuit in a graph. It avoids bridges unless no other option exists.

front: What is Hierholzer's algorithm used for?
back: Hierholzer's algorithm efficiently finds an Euler circuit in O(|E|) time by building cycles and splicing them together.

front: What was the Seven Bridges of Königsberg problem?
back: The problem asked whether one could walk through Königsberg crossing each of its seven bridges exactly once. Euler proved it impossible.

front: Define a bridge in graph theory
back: A bridge (or cut edge) is an edge whose removal disconnects the graph. Fleury's algorithm avoids traversing bridges unless no alternative exists.

front: How many vertices of odd degree can an Euler path start and end at?
back: An Euler path starts at one odd degree vertex and ends at the other odd degree vertex. Exactly two odd-degree vertices are allowed.

front: Can a disconnected graph have an Euler path or circuit?
back: No, only connected graphs can have Euler paths or circuits. The graph must be connected (or have exactly one non-trivial component).

front: What is the relationship between Eulerian and Semi-Eulerian graphs regarding vertex degrees?
back: Eulerian graphs have all vertices with even degree. Semi-Eulerian graphs have exactly two vertices with odd degree.

front: What is Fleury's algorithm's time complexity?
back: Fleury's algorithm has O(|E|²) time complexity because checking for bridges takes O(|E|) at each step.

front: State the handshaking lemma relation to Euler graphs
back: The handshaking lemma states sum of all vertex degrees = 2|E|. For Eulerian graphs, all degrees are even, so sum is even as expected.
